sources.title: Golavo deterministic engine · on-demand model council

verdict.text: The council makes the home side clear favourites at 54%, with the draw a distant second.

claims.text: The 54% home edge is not a lone signal — it rides on an 11-match unbeaten run and six clean sheets in the last eight, so the model's confidence and the form record point the same way.
Where the voices disagree is the draw: the goals model reads 1.7 expected goals for the hosts, high enough that a low-scoring stalemate is less likely than the raw 26% draw figure suggests.
The away side's route in is width and set-pieces rather than open play, and the goalscorer record shows their goals cluster among two forwards.

scenarios.text: If the hosts score first, the clean-sheet record makes a 1-0 or 2-0 grind the most probable finish; the 54% edge would firm further.
A level game past the hour tilts toward the away side's set-piece threat and, if it reaches penalties, a shootout — where the records are close.

research_notes.text: The two nations have met three times at previous World Cups, the hosts winning twice.
research_notes.quote: The teams have met on three occasions at the FIFA World Cup.
research_notes.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup — Wikipedia

research_notes.text: The tournament is the first to feature 48 teams, expanding the group stage.
research_notes.quote: The tournament will be the first to include 48 teams.
research_notes.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup — Wikipedia

background.text: Both managers favour a possession-based build-up, though this reflects the model's general knowledge and may be out of date.
